raw_input: A dark drawer contains 12 red socks and 12 blue socks, indistinguishable by touch. You pull socks out one at a time without looking. What is the MINIMUM number of socks you must pull to GUARANTEE having at least one matching pair (two socks of the same color)? Return a Python function named `answer` that takes no arguments and returns the minimum as an integer.
success_criteria: answer is exactly 3 (by pigeonhole: 2 socks could be one of each color; the 3rd must match one of them)

inline.tests:
got = answer()
assert isinstance(got, int), f'answer must be int, got {type(got).__name__}'
assert got == 3, f'expected 3, got {got}'
